Module 4:
Creating Prelaunch Content,
Part 3
!
Executive Summary
We're now going to talk about creating a screencap video, and the various
ways and types that you can use.
There are many different types of screencap:
• 	You can create a presentation in PowerPoint or in Keynote
• 	You can do a mind map or a process map
• 	You can also screencap a blog or a social media site - Example: go
through the comments and show people what others say/think about
you
• 	Email - you can do the same thing here
There are lots of tools you can use for this, and they keep on getting better.
Tools you can use:
• 	ScreenFlow -this is for Mac, and that’s what I’m using in this video
• 	Camtasia - for both Windows and Mac - expensive but there is a 30-
day free trial
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 2 --

• 	Quicktime - free and built right into your Mac
• 	CamStudio - I have no experience with it but you can check it out
• 	Audio is very important. You should always use an external mic - don’t
use your computer’s built-in mic as a general rule.
• 	Headset mics are cheap and they work great. The sound is consistent no
matter where you are vs. desktop mics that may change based on
environment. They are also generally cheaper and good quality.
• 	Make sure to get a USB headset so it just plugs right in to your
computer. When there is video I use a desktop mic so that you can’t see
a headset on me.
• 	A lot of times when I’m recording a screencap video, I want to be
standing up because I tend to have more energy this way.
• 	The desktop mic I like using is the Audio-Technica AT2020. As always
with electronics, there are many mics out there, although this one is
pretty inexpensive.
!
